topic: Introduction to Figurative Language: Personification, Rhyme, and Alliteration
title: What is Figurative Language? (Part 1)

questions.question: What does "figurative language" mean?
options: giving human qualities or actions to animals or objects (e.g., 'the grumpy sun').
using words in a creative way to mean something other than their literal meaning, to make writing more vivid.
words that have the same ending sound (e.g., 'bed' and 'head').
when words close together start with the same sound (e.g., 'happy house').
questions.correct_answer: using words in a creative way to mean something other than their literal meaning, to make writing more vivid.
questions.explanation: "figurative language" means: using words in a creative way to mean something other than their literal meaning, to make writing more vivid.

questions.question: What does "personification" mean?
options: using words in a creative way to mean something other than their literal meaning, to make writing more vivid.
when words close together start with the same sound (e.g., 'happy house').
giving human qualities or actions to animals or objects (e.g., 'the grumpy sun').
words that have the same ending sound (e.g., 'bed' and 'head').
questions.correct_answer: giving human qualities or actions to animals or objects (e.g., 'the grumpy sun').
questions.explanation: "personification" means: giving human qualities or actions to animals or objects (e.g., 'the grumpy sun').

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Figurative language ______ poetry engaging.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "Figurative language makes poetry engaging."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: Analyzing how poets use language helps us understand their message.
Rhyme creates musicality, and alliteration adds a pleasing sound effect.
Personification gives human traits to non-human things.
questions.correct_answer: Personification gives human traits to non-human things.
questions.explanation: Correct! "Personification gives human traits to non-human things." is one of the key things to remember.

questions.question: What does "rhyme" mean?
options: when words close together start with the same sound (e.g., 'happy house').
words that have the same ending sound (e.g., 'bed' and 'head').
using words in a creative way to mean something other than their literal meaning, to make writing more vivid.
giving human qualities or actions to animals or objects (e.g., 'the grumpy sun').
questions.correct_answer: words that have the same ending sound (e.g., 'bed' and 'head').
questions.explanation: "rhyme" means: words that have the same ending sound (e.g., 'bed' and 'head').
